coverage: 100% of reachable node types; the browser evaluates all 1,007 nodes in the root closure.
validation: Four Blender/VM cases cover authored settings, wrapper switching, thickness, and wrapper dimensions. Blender-float Newell face normals and safe-acos corner weights recover 38 authored vertices and make the authored face count exact: 61,697 / 53,892 versus Blender's 61,812 / 53,892, with bounds within 0.0303 units. Thickness 6 is 41,857 / 33,417 versus 42,031 / 33,434, but one outer Y extent remains 0.9862 units short; the 60 by 45 wrapper is 38,240 / 33,680 versus 38,301 / 33,678 and within 0.0100 units. Corrected multi-output nested probes establish the upstream boundary directly: shrink receives a bit-exact 57,757 / 57,276 mesh and Raycast selects exactly the same 52,555 hits as Blender. A source-exact float32 composition of the RELATIVE Object Info matrix makes all 387,348 target coordinate components bit-exact; with that probe, the first shrink/raycast branch, Separate Geometry, Join Geometry.003, and the BDSF group remain bit-exact. The first residual then occurs at the wrapper's final Set Position offset by point-domain Normal, with 0.0000038147 maximum position error. Matrix correction alone changes the final result to 61,667 / 53,894, so the public dump remains on the stronger 61,697 / 53,892 topology checkpoint. A tested Newell accumulation-order alternative worsened both normal RMS and final topology and was rejected. Grill therefore still needs downstream Normal/Set Position float-order reconciliation before adopting the exact relative matrix. Weld, proximity, autosmooth, normal tolerances, and epsilon snapping remain ruled out.
